summary: Protected Next.js public cache invalidation was added after successful admin mutations.
changed: Added /revalidate-public route with same-host and admin checks
Added public revalidation path and tag helpers
Added client helper for mutation-side revalidation calls
Tagged public fetches for immediate data cache invalidation
Wired blog, work, page, home, resume, and site settings mutations
Updated focused unit tests and Playwright waits for revalidation completion

validations.command: npx vitest run src/test/public-revalidation-paths.test.ts src/test/public-revalidation-route.test.ts src/test/blog-editor.test.tsx src/test/work-editor.test.tsx src/test/page-editor.test.tsx src/test/resume-editor.test.tsx src/test/inline-blog-editor-section.test.tsx src/test/inline-work-editor-section.test.tsx src/test/public-api-clients.test.ts src/test/public-api-contracts.test.ts --pool=threads

validations.details: Local Docker-owned .tmp/postgres directory caused EACCES during root ESLint scan

validations.command: npx eslint src tests playwright.config.ts next.config.ts eslint.config.mjs

risks: Root npm run lint remains blocked when local Docker .tmp/postgres is present and inaccessible
The route depends on the current public admin affordance/session helper
Tag invalidation uses revalidateTag(tag, 'max') and should be re-reviewed if Next cache semantics are changed
recommendation: Proceed with the focused change and follow up by excluding local Docker .tmp state from root ESLint scanning.
